zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske是Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq的女兒，於大約1505年出生在英國越可爾易亞斯比阿斯可。她於1521年7月8日與Sir Richard Bowes結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1568年在英國英格蘭達勒姆享年64歲，埋葬在英國達勒姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske是Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq的女儿，于大约1505年出生在英国越可爾易亞斯比阿斯可。她于1521年7月8日与Sir Richard Bowes结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1568年在英国英格兰達勒姆享年64岁，埋葬在英国達勒姆。

nameMeanings.name: Aske
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Staffordshire): topographic name from northern Middle English ask ‘ash tree’ (Old Norse askr), or a habitational name from Aske (North Yorkshire). Compare Ash .

variants.name: Ash
meanings: English: from Middle English asche ‘ash tree’ (Old English æsc), hence a topographic name for someone living by an ash tree or a habitational name from any of the many places in southern and central England named with this word (Derbyshire, Dorset, Hampshire, Herefordshire, Kent, Surrey, Shropshire, Somerset, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of German   Asch   and   Esch  , the latter ultimately also of Swiss German origin (see   Oesch  ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , with the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Axe
meanings: English (West Yorkshire, Lincolnshire): variant of Aske with metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/, spelled with an x.

English (Dorset, Somerset): habitational name from Axe Farm in Chardstock (Devon, formerly in Dorset), itself named for the river Axe, which forms part of the south-eastern boundary of Chardstock parish.
Americanized form of German   Axt   or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske was born about 1505, in Aske, Easby, Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq. She married Sir Richard Bowes on 8 July 1521. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1568, in Durham, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Durham, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske est née environ 1505, à Aske, Easby, Everwyck, Angleterre, fille de Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Bowes le 8 juillet 1521. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1568, à Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Durham, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Askeは約1505年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエアスビーアスクでSir Roger of Easby Aske Esqの娘として生まれた。1521年7月8日にSir Richard Bowesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1568年にイギリスイギリスダルハムで64歳で死去し，イギリスダルハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske(은)는 대략 1505년 영국 요르크실르 이스비 아스크에서 Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1521년 7월 8일 Sir Richard Bowes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1568년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 뒤람에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske родилась около 1505 г. в местности аск, эасби, еркшир, Англия. Её отца звали Sir Roger of Easby Aske Esq. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Bowes 8 июля 1521 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1568 г. в местности дурам, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности дурам, Англия.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1568, en Durham, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Durham, Inglaterra.
